Title or Description: ABA Model Rule 3.8 - Special Responsibilities of a Prosecutor
Proposition Supported: Professional-responsibility analogue for DOJ-009 because it identifies prosecutor-specific duties concerning unsupported charges, counsel access, disclosure, public comments, and remedial obligations.
Notes: Added during DOJ-009 T4; supports the heightened professional stakes for senior DOJ officials supervising prosecutorial authority.

Title or Description: Kennedy v. Braidwood Management, Inc.
Proposition Supported: Current Appointments Clause source for DOJ-009 because it restates the principal/inferior officer distinction and emphasizes direction, supervision, review, and removal as relevant to constitutional officer analysis.
Notes: Added during DOJ-009 T4; use as a caution source for principal-officer and acting-officer analysis, not as direct validation of proposed § 506A.

Title or Description: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure - December 1 2024
Proposition Supported: Official federal procedural source for ELEC-004 because it supplies current Rule 11 improper-purpose sanctions, Rule 26 protective-order authority, Rules 8 and 12 pleading/dismissal baseline, and printed title 28 rulemaking provisions used in the T4 follow-up legal-source validation.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; supersedes LII Rule 11 and Rule 26(c) rows SRC-0040 and SRC-0041 for active publication use. Verify whether a later official committee print has superseded the Dec. 1 2024 edition before final release.

Title or Description: Berk v. Choy
Proposition Supported: Current controlling federal-procedure source for ELEC-004 because it holds that Rules 8 and 12 displace a state-law affidavit-of-merit screen in federal court and therefore supports treating ELEC-004 as an express congressional statutory procedure rather than an inferred pleading gloss.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; use as a procedure-authority caution source, not as direct validation of every ELEC-004 screening element.

Proposition Supported: Primary statutory analogue for election-related expedited judicial procedure because subsection (d) establishes venue, three-judge panel, expedited handling, and direct Supreme Court review for certain certificate-of-ascertainment disputes.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; use as an expedited-election-procedure analogue, not as a direct Anti-SLAPP analogue.

Authority / Publisher: Justia / U.S. Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit
Title or Description: Trump v. Wisconsin Elections Commission, No. 20-3414
Proposition Supported: Reviewed opinion mirror supporting the Wisconsin manifestation: a federal post-certification challenge failed on delay/laches and Electors Clause grounds after seeking relief that would have affected Wisconsin's certified presidential result.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; replace or supplement with the official Seventh Circuit PDF before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: Bill Johnson's Restaurants, Inc. v. NLRB
Proposition Supported: Petition Clause and First Amendment source lead for ELEC-004 because it supports the proposition that baseless litigation is not immunized by the right to petition while preserving the need to avoid burdening legitimate lawsuits.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; verify against official U.S. Reports or Supreme Court source if available before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: BE&K Construction Co. v. NLRB
Proposition Supported: Petition Clause and First Amendment caution source for ELEC-004 because it recognizes First Amendment interests in reasonably based but unsuccessful lawsuits, supporting ELEC-004's no-automatic-penalty and nonfrivolous-claim safeguards.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; verify against official U.S. Reports or Supreme Court source if available before publication-ready reliance.

Proposition Supported: Primary statutory source for procedure-authority analysis because it governs submission and effective date of federal rules and includes examples of congressional modification, postponement, or displacement of proposed federal rule amendments by Act of Congress.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 T4 follow-up; use with 28 U.S.C. § 2072 and Berk v. Choy when analyzing whether the federal bill should be codified in title 28 or drafted as a freestanding procedure statute.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30104 - Reporting requirements
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA reporting source for ELEC-012 because it establishes federal campaign-finance reporting duties, electronic filing, public availability, and disclosure architecture that any post-ratification true-source statute should amend or conform to.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use for FECA codification and disclosure-system design.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30106 - Federal Election Commission
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA institutional source for ELEC-012 because it establishes FEC composition, party-balance limits, voting thresholds, staff authority, and civil-enforcement jurisdiction that affect FEC deadlock and contingent-safeguard design.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; verify official page access and pinpoint text before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30109 - Enforcement
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA enforcement source for ELEC-012 because it establishes administrative complaints, four-vote thresholds, conciliation, civil actions, and citizen-suit review after dismissal or failure to act.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use when conforming direct civil action and FEC-failure safeguards.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30116 - Limitations on contributions and expenditures
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA source for contribution-limit architecture relevant to super PAC contribution-cap experiments, coordination limits, anti-circumvention rules, and the post-SpeechNow ordinary-law baseline.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use with SpeechNow, NRSC, and Maine's Section 1015 comparator.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30118 - Corporate and labor-organization contributions or expenditures
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA source for corporate and labor-organization campaign-finance prohibitions and separately segregated fund concepts relevant to ELEC-012's artificial-entity election-spending design.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use with Citizens United and post-ratification artificial-entity rules.

Title or Description: 52 U.S.C. § 30121 - Contributions and donations by foreign nationals
Proposition Supported: Primary FECA source for foreign-national campaign-finance restrictions relevant to ELEC-012's foreign-influenced entity and anti-conduit safeguards.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use with Maine Section 1064 and foreign-money drafting.

Title or Description: Buckley v. Valeo
Proposition Supported: Campaign-finance baseline for ELEC-012 because it upheld contribution limits and disclosure while striking independent-expenditure limits and identifying the FEC appointment defect.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; replace or supplement with official U.S. Reports if practical before publication-ready reliance.

Title or Description: McCutcheon v. Federal Election Commission
Proposition Supported: Campaign-finance doctrine source for ELEC-012 because it limits anti-corruption interests to quid pro quo corruption or its appearance and treats disclosure and anti-circumvention as less restrictive tools than aggregate contribution caps.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use for disclosure, earmarking, anti-circumvention, and quid-pro-quo tailoring.

Title or Description: Federal Election Commission v. Ted Cruz for Senate
Proposition Supported: Campaign-finance doctrine source for ELEC-012 because it warns that speculative corruption theories and incumbent-protection effects cannot justify burdens on political speech.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use as a caution for loan, public-financing, and anti-corruption rationales.

Title or Description: Arizona Free Enterprise Club's Freedom Club PAC v. Bennett
Proposition Supported: Public-financing caution source for ELEC-012 because it invalidated opponent-triggered matching-fund structures that burdened nonparticipating speakers.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use before drafting small-donor matching, vouchers, grants, or spending-offset mechanisms.

Title or Description: Americans for Prosperity Foundation v. Bonta
Proposition Supported: Primary disclosure and associational-privacy caution source for ELEC-012 because it requires narrow tailoring for compelled donor disclosure and recognizes burdens involving threats, harassment, reprisals, and associational privacy.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use to calibrate true-source disclosure, harassment exceptions, confidential filings, and redaction/delay safeguards.

Title or Description: FEC approves two advisory opinions in wake of Citizens United and SpeechNow decisions
Proposition Supported: Official FEC source for the post-Citizens United and SpeechNow advisory-opinion response authorizing independent-expenditure-only committee structures and unlimited contributions for independent expenditures.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use as FEC implementation history for super PAC development.

Title or Description: Advisory Opinion 2010-11 - Commonsense Ten
Proposition Supported: Official FEC advisory-opinion source relevant to independent-expenditure-only committees and acceptance of contributions from individuals, political committees, corporations, and labor organizations for independent expenditures after Citizens United and SpeechNow.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use with AO 2010-09 and the FEC July 22 2010 release.

Title or Description: Advisory Opinion 2010-09 - Club for Growth
Proposition Supported: Official FEC advisory-opinion source relevant to independent-expenditure-only committee solicitation and acceptance of unlimited contributions from individuals for independent expenditures after Citizens United and SpeechNow.
Notes: Added during ELEC-012 T2 legal-durability audit; use with AO 2010-11 and the FEC July 22 2010 release.

Title or Description: Federal Prosecution of Election Offenses Seventh Edition May 2007 Revised August 2007
Proposition Supported: Official DOJ election-offenses manual source for ELEC-014 because it describes Public Integrity consultation for election fraud, patronage, and campaign-finance matters; limits preliminary inquiries; warns against pre-election voter interviews and overt election-related investigations that may chill voting; and requires Public Integrity consultation for grand-jury/full investigations and election-document subpoenas.
Notes: Added during ELEC-014 T1 issue-admission/source-development audit; historical manual should be compared against current Justice Manual 9-85 and any newer DOJ election-crimes guidance before publication-ready reliance.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. Code / House Office of the Law Revision Counsel
Title or Description: Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure
Proposition Supported: Current federal criminal-procedure baseline for ELEC-014 because Rules 3, 6, and 41 govern complaints, grand-jury secrecy/process, warrants, returns, suppression, and return of property relevant to election-related criminal searches and subpoenas.
Notes: Added during ELEC-014 T1 audit; T2 should pinpoint Rule 41 warrant requirements, Rule 6(e) secrecy, and any local-rule or sealed-docket limits.

Authority / Publisher: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia
Title or Description: Pearson v. Kemp, Transcript of Motions Hearing, Civil Action File No. 1:20-CV-4809-TCB
Proposition Supported: Primary transcript support for ELEC-004's Georgia source-development example because the hearing addressed emergency election-disruptive relief, inspection and access demands, decertification-style relief, and remedy-fit constraints.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 July 4 2026 targeted T4 source/comparator follow-up; CourtListener RECAP storage PDF was verified by direct extraction. Use narrowly as a hearing-transcript source, not as a written merits opinion.

Title or Description: Nevada Revised Statutes chapter 41, anti-SLAPP special motion provisions
Proposition Supported: Official state statutory comparator for ELEC-004 because NRS 41.660 and 41.670 provide a special motion to dismiss, discovery-stay architecture, fee-shifting, sanctions for frivolous or vexatious motions, and interlocutory appeal in Nevada's Anti-SLAPP framework.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 July 4 2026 targeted T4 source/comparator follow-up; use as a functional Anti-SLAPP comparator, not as an election-specific model.

Title or Description: Nevada Revised Statutes chapter 293, election contests and ballot-inspection limits
Proposition Supported: Official state statutory comparator for ELEC-004 because NRS 293.407, 293.410, 293.413, 293.424, and ballot-inspection provisions set election-contest filing, verification, materiality, expedited hearing, presidential-elector contest, special-master, and ballot-inspection rules.
Notes: Added during ELEC-004 July 4 2026 targeted T4 source/comparator follow-up; supports state-by-state tailoring of the model state bill.
